'''鳥鳴海神'''（とりなるみのかみ）は[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神]]。
== 概要 == [[古事記]]にのみ登場する国津神で、十七世神（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱。名前のみの記述で名義や神格は不明。
富家の系譜にも同名の神が見える。
== 系譜 == [[大国主神]]が[[八島士奴美神]]の娘[[鳥取神]]を娶って生んだ神で、[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]]を娶り[[国忍富神]]を生んだ。
== 祀る神社 == *成海神社 犬山市羽黒
== 関連項目 == *[[鳥取神]] *[[大国主神]] *[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] *[[国忍富神]] *[[出雲族]]
[[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]]

[[天津彦根命]]の9世孫に[[三上氏|三上直]]の祖として同名の'''鳥鳴海命'''が見える。系図によれば[[垂仁天皇]]二年夏四月に[[元伊勢|皇太神宮]]を[[伊賀国]]敢閉都美恵宮に遷坐し、次に同四年乙未六年晦に当国[[甲賀郡|甲可]]の日雲宮に遷坐して神御田を進め、その次に同八年己亥七年七日に同坂田宮に遷坐し二年あったとされる<ref>[[鈴木真年]]「東国諸国造 天津彦根之裔」『諸系譜』第一冊。</ref>。『古事記』で鳥鳴海命の子に置かれる[[国忍富命]]は、天津彦根命の7世孫で鳥鳴海命の祖父とされ、『古事記』で母とされる[[鳥耳命]]は、鳥鳴海命の甥で[[穴門国造]]の一人とされる。

== 祀る神社 == * [[成海神社（犬山市）|成海神社]]（[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒） * [[植木神社]]（[[三重県]][[伊賀市]]平田） * [[多比鹿神社]]（三重県[[三重郡]][[菰野町]]）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[鳥取神]] *[[大国主神]] *[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] *[[国忍富神]] *[[出雲族]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とりなるみのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:伊賀国の人物]] [[Category:近江国の人物]] [[Category:三上氏]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 鳥鳴海神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第七代 | 先代 = [[大国主神]] | 次代 = [[国忍富神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 鳥鳴海神 | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->神事 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[大国主神]] | 母 = [[鳥取神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 子 = [[国忍富神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[多比鹿神社]]等 }} {{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 鳥鳴海命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[垂仁天皇]] | 氏族 = [[三上氏|三上直]]祖 | 父母 = 父：大加賀美命 | 兄弟 = 速都鳥命 | 妻 = | 子 = 八倉田命 | 特記事項 = }}
'''鳥鳴海神'''（とりなるみのかみ）・'''鳥鳴海命'''（とりなるみのみこと）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]、または[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』にのみ登場する国津神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱。名前のみの記述である。
名義は「鳥が霊を運ぶ、鳴り響く海」と考えられている<ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[新潮社]]、2014年、381頁。</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘[[鳥取神]]を娶って生んだ神で、[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]]を娶り[[国忍富神]]を生んだ。
== 祀る神社 == * [[成海神社（犬山市）|成海神社]]（[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒） * [[植木神社]]（[[三重県]][[伊賀市]]平田） * [[多比鹿神社]]（三重県[[三重郡]][[菰野町]]）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[鳥取神]] *[[大国主神]] *[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] *[[国忍富神]] *[[出雲族]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are..."
revision.text:
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.

This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 鳥鳴海神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第七代 | 先代 = [[大国主神]] | 次代 = [[国忍富神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 鳥鳴海神（トリナルミノカミ） | 別名 = 鳥鳴海命 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = [[大国主神]] | 母 = [[鳥取神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 子 = [[国忍富神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * 成海神社 * 植木神社 * 多比鹿神社 }} | 記紀等 = 古事記 }} '''鳥鳴海神'''（鳥鳴海命）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』にのみ登場する国津神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱。名前のみの記述である。
名義は「鳥が霊を運ぶ、鳴り響く海」と考えられている<ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[新潮社]]、2014年、381頁。</ref>。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] 大国主神が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘鳥取神を娶って生んだ神で、日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神を娶り国忍富神を生んだ。 == 祀る神社 == * 成海神社（[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒八幡東） * 植木神社（[[三重県]][[伊賀市]]平田） * 多比鹿神社（三重県[[三重郡]][[菰野町]]田光） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[鳥取神]] *[[大国主神]] *[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] *[[国忍富神]] *[[出雲族]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とりなるみのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 鳥鳴海神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第七代 | 先代 = [[大国主神]] | 次代 = [[国忍富神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 鳥鳴海神（トリナルミノカミ） | 別名 = 鳥鳴海命 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->神事の神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[大国主神]] | 母 = [[鳥取神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 子 = [[国忍富神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * 成海神社 * 植木神社 * 多比鹿神社 }} | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''鳥鳴海神'''（鳥鳴海命）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』にのみ登場する国津神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱。名前のみの記述である。
名義は「鳥が霊を運ぶ、鳴り響く海」と考えられている<ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[新潮社]]、2014年、381頁。</ref>。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] 大国主神が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘鳥取神を娶って生んだ神で、日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神を娶り国忍富神を生んだ。 == 祀る神社 == * 成海神社（[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒八幡東） - 主祭神 * 植木神社（[[三重県]][[伊賀市]]平田） - 配祀 * 多比鹿神社（三重県[[三重郡]][[菰野町]]田光） - 合祀 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[出雲族]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/torinaruminokami/ 鳥鳴海神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [https://www.city.inuyama.aichi.jp/kurashi/bunka/1001063/1001069.html 寺社一覧｜犬山市] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e6%a4%8d%e6%9c%a8%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 三重県神社庁教化委員会 » 植木神社] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 三重県神社庁教化委員会 » 多比鹿神社] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とりなるみのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

{{改名提案|トリナルミ|t=ノート:十七世神|date=2024年8月}} {{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 鳥鳴海神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第七代 | 先代 = [[大国主神]] | 次代 = [[国忍富神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 鳥鳴海神（トリナルミノカミ） | 別名 = 鳥鳴海命 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->神事の神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[大国主神]] | 母 = [[鳥取神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 子 = [[国忍富神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * 成海神社 * 植木神社 * 多比鹿神社 }} | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''鳥鳴海神'''（鳥鳴海命）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』にのみ登場する国津神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱。名前のみの記述である。
名義は「鳥が霊を運ぶ、鳴り響く海」と考えられている<ref>[[西宮一民]]「付録 神名の釈義」『新潮日本古典集成 古事記』[[新潮社]]、2014年、381頁。</ref>。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] 大国主神が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘鳥取神を娶って生んだ神で、日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神を娶り国忍富神を生んだ。 == 祀る神社 == * 成海神社（[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒八幡東） - 主祭神 * 植木神社（[[三重県]][[伊賀市]]平田） - 配祀 * 多比鹿神社（三重県[[三重郡]][[菰野町]]田光） - 合祀 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[出雲族]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/torinaruminokami/ 鳥鳴海神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [https://www.city.inuyama.aichi.jp/kurashi/bunka/1001063/1001069.html 寺社一覧｜犬山市] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e6%a4%8d%e6%9c%a8%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 三重県神社庁教化委員会 » 植木神社] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 三重県神社庁教化委員会 » 多比鹿神社] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とりなるみのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 鳥鳴海神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第七代 | 先代 = [[大国主神]] | 次代 = [[国忍富神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 鳥鳴海神（トリナルミノカミ） | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->神事の神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[大国主神]] | 母 = [[鳥取神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 子 = [[国忍富神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * 成海神社 * 植木神社 * 多比鹿神社 }} | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''鳥鳴海神'''（トリナルミ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』にのみ登場する国津神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱。名前のみの記述である。
名義は「鳥が霊を運ぶ、鳴り響く海」と考えられている{{Sfn|西宮|2014|p=381}}。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] 大国主神が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘鳥取神を娶って生んだ神で、日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神を娶り国忍富神を生んだ。 == 祀る神社 == * 成海神社（[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒八幡東） - 主祭神 * 植木神社（[[三重県]][[伊賀市]]平田） - 配祀 * 多比鹿神社（三重県[[三重郡]][[菰野町]]田光） - 合祀 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * <!-- にしみや -->{{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=2014年10月30日 |publisher=[[新潮社]] |ref={{SfnRef|西宮|2014}} |author=[[西宮一民]] 校注 |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series=[[新潮日本古典集成]]（新装版）}} == 関連項目 == *[[カヤナルミ]] - 『[[延喜式]]』巻八の「[[出雲国造神賀詞]]」に登場する神。同じくナルミを名に持つことから関連性が考察されている。 *[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]] - 十七世神の第十三代。 == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/torinaruminokami/ 鳥鳴海神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [https://www.city.inuyama.aichi.jp/kurashi/bunka/1001063/1001069.html 寺社一覧｜犬山市] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e6%a4%8d%e6%9c%a8%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 三重県神社庁教化委員会 » 植木神社] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 三重県神社庁教化委員会 » 多比鹿神社] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とりなるみのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

revision.comment: 2 revisions imported: importing my simple english wikipedia drafts for the 17 spirits of the world
revision.text:
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. revision.text:
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories|[[Category:recently imported simple english 17 spirits of the world]] {{ill|Category:17 spirits of the world|ja|Category:十七世神|fr|Category:17 esprits du monde}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] }}

(2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). [[ja:鳥鳴海神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). [[ja:鳥鳴海神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

(daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}). [[ja:鳥鳴海神]] This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories|[[Category:Recently imported simple english 17 spirits of the world]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami]]{{ill|Category:17 spirits of the world|ja|Category:十七世神|fr|Category:17 esprits du monde}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] }}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|goodbye $1/17 spirits of the world|en]][[ja:十七世神|fr|goodbye $1/17 esprits du monde]] | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|goodbye $1/17 spirits of the world|en]][[ja:十七世神|fr|goodbye $1/17 esprits du monde]]. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en]]) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|en]][[ja:建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto]].goodbye $1 | Name = Torinaruminokami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|en]][[ja:十七世神]] (7th Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en]][[ja:大国主神]] | Successor = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi no Kami|en]][[ja:国忍富神]] | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|en]][[ja:国津神]] | Full Name = 鳥鳴海神 (Torinaruminokami) | Alternate Name= | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of Rituals | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en]][[ja:大国主神]] | mother = {{ill|Tottorijin|en]][[ja:鳥取神]] | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Hinateru Nukatabimichioikochini no Kami|en]][[ja:日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | children = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi no Kami|en]][[ja:国忍富神]] | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi Shrine * Ueki Shrine * Tahika Shrine ]] | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en]][[ja:古事記]] ]]
'''Torinaruminokami''' (鳥鳴海神, トリナルミ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en]][[ja:日本神話]].
== Overview == Torinaruminokami is a deity mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en]][[ja:古事記]]'' as one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|en]][[ja:十七世神]] (Tōmarinanayo no Kami). The deity's role and actions are not elaborated beyond the name, which is interpreted to mean "a sea where birds carry spirits and resound."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381]]
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Torinaruminokami was born to {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en]][[ja:大国主神]] and {{ill|Tottorijin|en]][[ja:鳥取神]]. The deity married {{ill|Hinateru Nukatabimichioikochini no Kami|en]][[ja:日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] and fathered {{ill|Kuninotoshimi no Kami|en]][[ja:国忍富神]].
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama|en]][[ja:犬山市]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en]][[ja:愛知県]]) - Main enshrined deity. * '''Ueki Shrine''' ({{ill|Iga|en]][[ja:伊賀市]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en]][[ja:三重県]]) - Secondary enshrined deity. * '''Tahika Shrine''' ({{ill|Komono Town|en]][[ja:菰野町]], {{ill|Mie District, Mie Prefecture|en]][[ja:三重郡]], Mie Prefecture) - Merged deity.
== Notes == {{Help footnotes]]
=== Sources === {{Reflist]]
== References == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=October 30, 2014 |publisher={{ill|Shinchosha|en]][[ja:新潮社]] |ref={{SfnRef|Nishinomiya|2014]] |author={{ill|Kazutami Nishinomiya|en]][[ja:西宮一民]] (Editor) |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series={{ill|Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium|en]][[ja:新潮日本古典集成]] (New Edition)]]
== See Also == * {{ill|Kayanarumi|en]][[ja:カヤナルミ]] - A deity mentioned in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en]][[ja:延喜式]]'' (Volume 8, ''Izumo no Kuni no Kami no Norito''), considered related due to the shared name element "Narumi." * {{ill|Nunobutomi Torinaruminokami|en]][[ja:布忍富鳥鳴海神]] - The 13th generation of the 17 Generations of Deities.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/torinaruminokami/ Torinaruminokami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://www.city.inuyama.aichi.jp/kurashi/bunka/1001063/1001069.html List of Temples and Shrines in Inuyama | Inuyama City] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e6%a4%8d%e6%9c%a8%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Ueki Shrine | Mie Shrine Association Education Committee] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tahika Shrine | Mie Shrine Association Education Committee]

This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|en}}[[ja:建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto]].goodbye $1 | Name = Torinaruminokami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|en}}[[ja:十七世神]] (7th Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[ja:大国主神]] | Successor = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi no Kami|en}}[[ja:国忍富神]] | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}[[ja:国津神]] | Full Name = 鳥鳴海神 (Torinaruminokami) | Alternate Name= | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of Rituals | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[ja:大国主神]] | mother = {{ill|Tottorijin|en}}[[ja:鳥取神]] | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Hinateru Nukatabimichioikochini no Kami|en}}[[ja:日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | children = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi no Kami|en}}[[ja:国忍富神]] | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi Shrine * Ueki Shrine * Tahika Shrine ]] | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en}}[[ja:古事記]] ]]
'''Torinaruminokami''' (鳥鳴海神, トリナルミ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en}}[[ja:日本神話]].
== Overview == Torinaruminokami is a deity mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en}}[[ja:古事記]]'' as one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|en}}[[ja:十七世神]] (Tōmarinanayo no Kami). The deity's role and actions are not elaborated beyond the name, which is interpreted to mean "a sea where birds carry spirits and resound."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381]]
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Torinaruminokami was born to {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[ja:大国主神]] and {{ill|Tottorijin|en}}[[ja:鳥取神]]. The deity married {{ill|Hinateru Nukatabimichioikochini no Kami|en}}[[ja:日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] and fathered {{ill|Kuninotoshimi no Kami|en}}[[ja:国忍富神]].
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama|en}}[[ja:犬山市]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en}}[[ja:愛知県]]) - Main enshrined deity. * '''Ueki Shrine''' ({{ill|Iga|en}}[[ja:伊賀市]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) - Secondary enshrined deity. * '''Tahika Shrine''' ({{ill|Komono Town|en}}[[ja:菰野町]], {{ill|Mie District, Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重郡]], Mie Prefecture) - Merged deity.
== Notes == {{Help footnotes]]
=== Sources === {{Reflist]]
== References == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=October 30, 2014 |publisher={{ill|Shinchosha|en}}[[ja:新潮社]] |ref={{SfnRef|Nishinomiya|2014]] |author={{ill|Kazutami Nishinomiya|en}}[[ja:西宮一民]] (Editor) |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series={{ill|Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium|en}}[[ja:新潮日本古典集成]] (New Edition)]]
== See Also == * {{ill|Kayanarumi|en}}[[ja:カヤナルミ]] - A deity mentioned in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en}}[[ja:延喜式]]'' (Volume 8, ''Izumo no Kuni no Kami no Norito''), considered related due to the shared name element "Narumi." * {{ill|Nunobutomi Torinaruminokami|en}}[[ja:布忍富鳥鳴海神]] - The 13th generation of the 17 Generations of Deities.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/torinaruminokami/ Torinaruminokami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://www.city.inuyama.aichi.jp/kurashi/bunka/1001063/1001069.html List of Temples and Shrines in Inuyama | Inuyama City] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e6%a4%8d%e6%9c%a8%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Ueki Shrine | Mie Shrine Association Education Committee] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tahika Shrine | Mie Shrine Association Education Committee]

This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|en}}[[ja:建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto]].goodbye $1 | Name = Torinaruminokami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|en}}[[ja:十七世神]] (7th Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[ja:大国主神]] | Successor = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi no Kami|en}}[[ja:国忍富神]] | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}[[ja:国津神]] | Full Name = 鳥鳴海神 (Torinaruminokami) | Alternate Name= | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of Rituals | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[ja:大国主神]] | mother = {{ill|Tottorijin|en}}[[ja:鳥取神]] | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Hinateru Nukatabimichioikochini no Kami|en}}[[ja:日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | children = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi no Kami|en}}[[ja:国忍富神]] | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi Shrine * Ueki Shrine * Tahika Shrine}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en}}[[ja:古事記]] }}
'''Torinaruminokami''' (鳥鳴海神, トリナルミ) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en}}[[ja:日本神話]].
== Overview == Torinaruminokami is a deity mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en}}[[ja:古事記]]'' as one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|en}}[[ja:十七世神]] (Tōmarinanayo no Kami). The deity's role and actions are not elaborated beyond the name, which is interpreted to mean "a sea where birds carry spirits and resound."{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=381]]
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Torinaruminokami was born to {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[ja:大国主神]] and {{ill|Tottorijin|en}}[[ja:鳥取神]]. The deity married {{ill|Hinateru Nukatabimichioikochini no Kami|en}}[[ja:日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] and fathered {{ill|Kuninotoshimi no Kami|en}}[[ja:国忍富神]].
== Shrines Dedicated == * '''Narumi Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama|en}}[[ja:犬山市]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en}}[[ja:愛知県]]) - Main enshrined deity. * '''Ueki Shrine''' ({{ill|Iga|en}}[[ja:伊賀市]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重県]]) - Secondary enshrined deity. * '''Tahika Shrine''' ({{ill|Komono Town|en}}[[ja:菰野町]], {{ill|Mie District, Mie Prefecture|en}}[[ja:三重郡]], Mie Prefecture) - Merged deity.
== Notes == {{Help footnotes]]
=== Sources === {{Reflist]]
== References == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=October 30, 2014 |publisher={{ill|Shinchosha|en}}[[ja:新潮社]] |ref={{SfnRef|Nishinomiya|2014]] |author={{ill|Kazutami Nishinomiya|en}}[[ja:西宮一民]] (Editor) |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series={{ill|Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium|en}}[[ja:新潮日本古典集成]] (New Edition)]]
== See Also == * {{ill|Kayanarumi|en}}[[ja:カヤナルミ]] - A deity mentioned in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en}}[[ja:延喜式]]'' (Volume 8, ''Izumo no Kuni no Kami no Norito''), considered related due to the shared name element "Narumi." * {{ill|Nunobutomi Torinaruminokami|en}}[[ja:布忍富鳥鳴海神]] - The 13th generation of the 17 Generations of Deities.
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/torinaruminokami/ Torinaruminokami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [https://www.city.inuyama.aichi.jp/kurashi/bunka/1001063/1001069.html List of Temples and Shrines in Inuyama | Inuyama City] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e6%a4%8d%e6%9c%a8%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Ueki Shrine | Mie Shrine Association Education Committee] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tahika Shrine | Mie Shrine Association Education Committee]

This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|en}}[[ja:建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto]].goodbye $1 | Name = Torinaruminokami | Generation = {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}}[[ja:十七世神]] (7th Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[ja:大国主神]] | Successor = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi no Kami|en}}[[ja:国忍富神]] | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}[[ja:国津神]] | Full Name = 鳥鳴海神 (Torinaruminokami) | Alternate Name= | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of Rituals | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[ja:大国主神]] | mother = {{ill|Tottorijin|en}}[[ja:鳥取神]] | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Hinateru Nukatabimichioikochini no Kami|en}}[[ja:日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | children = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi no Kami|en}}[[ja:国忍富神]] | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi Shrine * Ueki Shrine * Tahika Shrine}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en}}[[ja:古事記]] }}
'''Torinarumi-no-Kami''' (鳥鳴海神, トリナルミ, {{lang-en|Torinarumi-no-Kami}}) is a {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|deity in Japanese mythology=deity in Japanese mythology|lt=deity in Japanese mythology}}.
== Overview == Torinarumi-no-Kami is a deity mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and belongs to the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} (とおまりななよのかみ). This deity is classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} and is only described by name in the text. The name is interpreted as "a bird that carries spirits, resonating through the sea"{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=381}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red female deities, and yellow indicates unknown gender.]] Torinarumi-no-Kami was born from the union of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} and his wife, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja|鳥取神}}, the daughter of {{ill|Yashimajin|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|1=Yashimamuji}}. Torinarumi-no-Kami married {{ill|Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami}} and gave birth to {{ill|Kuninobutomi-no-Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi no Kami}}.
== Shrines enshrining Torinarumi-no-Kami == * '''Narumi Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama|ja|犬山市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) - Main deity * '''Ueki Shrine''' ({{ill|Iga|ja|伊賀市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) - Enshrined alongside others * '''Tabika Shrine''' (Komono Town, {{ill|Mie District, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重郡}}) - Included in a [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merger]]
*

'''Torinarumi-no-Kami''' (鳥鳴海神, トリナルミ, {{lang-en|Torinarumi-no-Kami}}) is a {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|deity in Japanese mythology=deity in Japanese mythology|lt=deity in Japanese mythology}}.
== Overview == Torinarumi-no-Kami is a deity mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and belongs to the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} (とおまりななよのかみ). This deity is classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} and is only described by name in the text. The name is interpreted as "a bird that carries spirits, resonating through the sea"{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=381}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red female deities, and yellow indicates unknown gender.]] Torinarumi-no-Kami was born from the union of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} and his wife, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja|鳥取神}}, the daughter of {{ill|Yashimajin|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|1=Yashimamuji}}. Torinarumi-no-Kami married {{ill|Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami}} and gave birth to {{ill|Kuninobutomi-no-Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi no Kami}}.

[[ja:%E9%B3%A5%E9%B3%B4%E6%B5%B7%E7%A5%9E]] {{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|goodbye $1/17 spirits of the world|en}}[[ja:十七世神|fr|goodbye $1/17 esprits du monde]]. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|en}}[[ja:建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto]].goodbye $1 | Name = Torinaruminokami | Generation = {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}}[[ja:十七世神]] (7th Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[ja:大国主神]] | Successor = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|en}}[[ja:国忍富神]] | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}[[ja:国津神]] | Full Name = 鳥鳴海神 (Torinaruminokami) | Alternate Name= | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of Rituals | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[ja:大国主神]] | mother = {{ill|Tottorijin|en}}[[ja:鳥取神]] | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Hinateru Nukatabimichioikochini no Kami|en}}[[ja:日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | children = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|en}}[[ja:国忍富神]] | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi Shrine * Ueki Shrine * Tahika Shrine}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en}}[[ja:古事記]] }}
'''Torinarumi-no-Kami''' (鳥鳴海神, トリナルミ, {{lang-en|Torinarumi-no-Kami}}) is a {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|deity in Japanese mythology=deity in Japanese mythology|lt=deity in Japanese mythology}}.
== Overview == Torinarumi-no-Kami is a deity mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and belongs to the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} (とおまりななよのかみ). This deity is classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} and is only described by name in the text. The name is interpreted as "a bird that carries spirits, resonating through the sea"{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=381}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red female deities, and yellow indicates unknown gender.]] Torinarumi-no-Kami was born from the union of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} and his wife, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja|鳥取神}}, the daughter of {{ill|Yashimajin|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|1=Yashimamuji}}. Torinarumi-no-Kami married {{ill|Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami}} and gave birth to {{ill|Kuninobutomi-no-Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami}}.
== Shrines enshrining Torinarumi-no-Kami == * '''Narumi Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama|ja|犬山市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) - Main deity * '''Ueki Shrine''' ({{ill|Iga|ja|伊賀市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) - Enshrined alongside others * '''Tabika Shrine''' (Komono Town, {{ill|Mie District, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重郡}}) - Included in a [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merger]]
*

[[ja:%E9%B3%A5%E9%B3%B4%E6%B5%B7%E7%A5%9E]] {{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|goodbye $1/17 spirits of the world|en}}[[ja:十七世神|fr|goodbye $1/17 esprits du monde]]. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|en}}[[ja:建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto]].goodbye $1 | Name = Torinaruminokami | Generation = {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}}[[ja:十七世神]] (7th Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[ja:大国主神]] | Successor = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|en}}[[ja:国忍富神]] | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}[[ja:国津神]] | Full Name = 鳥鳴海神 (Torinaruminokami) | Alternate Name= | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of Rituals | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}[[ja:大国主神]] | mother = {{ill|Tottorijin|en}}[[ja:鳥取神]] | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Hinateru Nukatabimichioikochini no Kami|en}}[[ja:日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | children = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|en}}[[ja:国忍富神]] | cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi Shrine * Ueki Shrine * Tahika Shrine}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en}}[[ja:古事記]] }}
'''Torinarumi-no-Kami''' (鳥鳴海神, トリナルミ, {{lang-en|Torinarumi-no-Kami}}) is a {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|deity in Japanese mythology=deity in Japanese mythology|lt=deity in Japanese mythology}}.
== Overview == Torinarumi-no-Kami is a deity mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and belongs to the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} (とおまりななよのかみ). This deity is classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} and is only described by name in the text. The name is interpreted as "a bird that carries spirits, resonating through the sea"{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=381}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red female deities, and yellow indicates unknown gender.]] Torinarumi-no-Kami was born from the union of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} and his wife, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja|鳥取神}}, the daughter of {{ill|Yashimajin|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|1=Yashimamuji}}. Torinarumi-no-Kami married {{ill|Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami}} and gave birth to {{ill|Kuninobutomi-no-Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami}}.
== Shrines enshrining Torinarumi-no-Kami == * '''Narumi Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama|ja|犬山市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) - Main deity * '''Ueki Shrine''' ({{ill|Iga|ja|伊賀市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) - Enshrined alongside others * '''Tabika Shrine''' (Komono Town, {{ill|Mie District, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重郡}}) - Included in a [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merger]]
*

This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|en|12=draft}}.goodbye $1 | Name = Torinaruminokami | Generation = {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|12=draft}} (7th Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}|Successor = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|en|12=draft}} | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}|Full Name = 鳥鳴海神 (Torinaruminokami) | Alternate Name= | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of Rituals | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=draft}} | mother = {{ill|Tottorijin|en}}|Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Hinateru Nukatabimichioikochini no Kami|en|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|en}}|cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi Shrine * Ueki Shrine * Tahika Shrine|12=draft}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en}} }}
'''Torinarumi-no-Kami''' (鳥鳴海神, トリナルミ, {{lang-en|Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=draft}}) is a {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|deity in Japanese mythology=deity in Japanese mythology|lt=deity in Japanese mythology|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Torinarumi-no-Kami is a deity mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} and belongs to the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|12=draft}} (とおまりななよのかみ). This deity is classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|12=draft}} and is only described by name in the text. The name is interpreted as "a bird that carries spirits, resonating through the sea"{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=381}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red female deities, and yellow indicates unknown gender.]] Torinarumi-no-Kami was born from the union of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=draft}} and his wife, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja|鳥取神|12=draft}}, the daughter of {{ill|Yashimajin|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|1=Yashimamuji|12=draft}}. Torinarumi-no-Kami married {{ill|Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=draft}} and gave birth to {{ill|Kuninobutomi-no-Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|12=draft}}.
== Shrines enshrining Torinarumi-no-Kami == * '''Narumi Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama|ja|犬山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}) - Main deity * '''Ueki Shrine''' ({{ill|Iga|ja|伊賀市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}) - Enshrined alongside others * '''Tabika Shrine''' (Komono Town, {{ill|Mie District, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重郡|12=draft}}) - Included in a [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merger]]
*
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|Kayanarumi|ja|カヤナルミ|12=draft}} - A deity appearing in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}'' (巻八, ''Izumo Province Norito''), thought to have a connection due to the shared element "Narumi." * {{ill|Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=draft}} - The thirteenth generation deity among the Completion tier 3]] |ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}} |author=[[Kazutami Nishimiya]] 校注 |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series=[[Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei]] (new edition)}}

This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|en|12=draft}}.goodbye $1 | Name = Torinaruminokami | Generation = {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|12=draft}} (7th Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}}|Successor = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|en|12=draft}} | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}|Full Name = 鳥鳴海神 (Torinaruminokami) | Alternate Name= | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = God of Rituals | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=draft}} | mother = {{ill|Tottorijin|en}}|Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Hinateru Nukatabimichioikochini no Kami|en|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|en}}|cult_centre = {{Plainlist| * Narumi Shrine * Ueki Shrine * Tahika Shrine|12=draft}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en}} }}
'''Torinarumi-no-Kami''' (鳥鳴海神, トリナルミ, {{lang-en|Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=draft}}) is a {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|deity in Japanese mythology=deity in Japanese mythology|lt=deity in Japanese mythology|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Torinarumi-no-Kami is a deity mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} and belongs to the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|12=draft}} (とおまりななよのかみ). This deity is classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|12=draft}} and is only described by name in the text. The name is interpreted as "a bird that carries spirits, resonating through the sea"{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=381}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red female deities, and yellow indicates unknown gender.]] Torinarumi-no-Kami was born from the union of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=draft}} and his wife, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja|鳥取神|12=draft}}, the daughter of {{ill|Yashimajin|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|1=Yashimamuji|12=draft}}. Torinarumi-no-Kami married {{ill|Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=draft}} and gave birth to {{ill|Kuninobutomi-no-Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|12=draft}}.
== Shrines enshrining Torinarumi-no-Kami == * '''Narumi Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama|ja|犬山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}) - Main deity * '''Ueki Shrine''' ({{ill|Iga|ja|伊賀市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}) - Enshrined alongside others * '''Tabika Shrine''' (Komono Town, {{ill|Mie District, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重郡|12=draft}}) - Included in a [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merger]]
*
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|Kayanarumi|ja|カヤナルミ|12=draft}} - A deity appearing in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}'' (巻八, ''Izumo Province Norito''), thought to have a connection due to the shared element "Narumi." * {{ill|Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=draft}} - The thirteenth generation deity among the Completion tier 3]] |ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}} |author=[[Kazutami Nishimiya]] 校注 |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series=[[Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei]] (new edition)}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]]

== Overview == Torinarumi-no-Kami is a deity mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} and belongs to the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|12=draft}} (とおまりななよのかみ). This deity is classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|12=draft}} and is only described by name in the text. The name is interpreted as "a bird that carries spirits, resonating through the sea"{{Sfn|Nishimiya|2014|p=381}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red female deities, and yellow indicates unknown gender.]] Torinarumi-no-Kami was born from the union of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=draft}} and his wife, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|ja|鳥取神|12=draft}}, the daughter of {{ill|Yashimajin|ja|八島牟遅能神|1=Yashimamuji|1=Yashimamuji|12=draft}}. Torinarumi-no-Kami married {{ill|Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=draft}} and gave birth to {{ill|Kuninobutomi-no-Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|12=draft}}.
== Shrines enshrining Torinarumi-no-Kami == * '''Narumi Shrine''' ({{ill|Inuyama|ja|犬山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}) - Main deity * '''Ueki Shrine''' ({{ill|Iga|ja|伊賀市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}) - Enshrined alongside others * '''Tabika Shrine''' (Komono Town, {{ill|Mie District, Mie Prefecture|ja|三重郡|12=draft}}) - Included in a [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merger]]
*
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|Kayanarumi|ja|カヤナルミ|12=draft}} - A deity appearing in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}'' (巻八, ''Izumo Province Norito''), thought to have a connection due to the shared element "Narumi." * {{ill|Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|1=Nunobutomi-Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=draft}} - The thirteenth generation deity among the Completion tier 3]] |ref={{SfnRef|Nishimiya|2014}} |author=[[Kazutami Nishimiya]] 校注 |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series=[[Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei]] (new edition)}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]]
== Notes ==
=== References === {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=2014th year 10月30日 |publisher=[[Shinchōsha]] [[Category:Recently imported simple english 17 spirits of the world]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Torinaruminokami]] [[Category:17 spirits of the world]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fix draft categories]] }} [[ja:%E9%B3%A5%E9%B3%B4%E6%B5%B7%E7%A5%9E]] [[ja:十七世神|fr|goodbye $1/17 esprits du monde]] [[ja:建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto]] [[ja:十七世神]] [[ja:大国主神]] [[ja:国忍富神]] [[ja:国津神]] [[ja:鳥取神]] [[ja:日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] [[ja:古事記]] [[ja:鳥鳴海神]] [[sn:Torinaruminokami]] [[sn:ategory:十七世神]] [[sn:Torinarumi-no-Kami]] [[sn:orinaruminokami]] == External links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/torinaruminokami/ Torinarumi-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Classic Culture Project] * [https://www.city.inuyama.aichi.jp/kurashi/bunka/1001063/1001069.html List of Shrines | Inuyama City] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e6%a4%8d%e6%9c%a8%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Mie Prefecture Shrine Association » Ueki Shrine] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Mie Prefecture Shrine Association » Tabika Shrine]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 鳥鳴海神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第七代 | 先代 = [[大国主神]] | 次代 = [[国忍富神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 鳥鳴海神（トリナルミノカミ） | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->神事の神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[大国主神]] | 母 = [[鳥取神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 子 = [[国忍富神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * 成海神社 * 植木神社 * 多比鹿神社 }} | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''鳥鳴海神'''（トリナルミ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』にのみ登場する国津神で、[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱。名前のみの記述である。
名義は「鳥が霊を運ぶ、鳴り響く海」と考えられている{{Sfn|西宮|2014|p=381}}。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] 大国主神が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘鳥取神を娶って生んだ神で、日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神を娶り国忍富神を生んだ。 == 祀る神社 == * 成海神社（[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]羽黒八幡東） - 主祭神 * 植木神社（[[三重県]][[伊賀市]]平田） - 配祀 * 多比鹿神社（三重県[[三重郡]][[菰野町]]田光） - 合祀 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * <!-- にしみや -->{{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=2014年10月30日 |publisher=[[新潮社]] |ref={{SfnRef|西宮|2014}} |author=[[西宮一民]] 校注 |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series=[[新潮日本古典集成]]（新装版）}} == 関連項目 == *[[カヤナルミ]] - 『[[延喜式]]』巻八の「[[出雲国造神賀詞]]」に登場する神。同じくナルミを名に持つことから関連性が考察されている。 *[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]] - 十七世神の第十三代。 == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/torinaruminokami/ 鳥鳴海神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [https://www.city.inuyama.aichi.jp/kurashi/bunka/1001063/1001069.html 寺社一覧｜犬山市] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e6%a4%8d%e6%9c%a8%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 三重県神社庁教化委員会 » 植木神社] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 三重県神社庁教化委員会 » 多比鹿神社] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とりなるみのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:十七世神]]
